assertion_results.text: Test Quality verdict is WARN (not N/A or PASS) — the Eval Quality component is WARN because eval-3 has 2 failing assertions, and WARN in any component produces WARN in the combined Test Quality verdict
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13: 'Test Quality | WARN | Eval Quality: WARN -- eval-3 has 2 failing assertions (85% pass rate); overall eval pass rate 91% (54/56 assertions). Repetitive Test Detection: N/A. Test Documentation: N/A.' The Test Quality verdict is WARN, driven by the Eval Quality component being WARN due to eval-3's 2 failing assertions.

assertion_results.text: The eval result review from github-actions[bot] is correctly identified as an eval result — the report's Test Quality or Eval Quality section reflects eval metrics (pass rates, failing assertions) extracted from the eval review, confirming the orchestrator detected and processed it
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 27-30: 'Review 40001 from github-actions[bot] was correctly identified as an eval result review by matching all three detection criteria: 1. Author is github-actions[bot] 2. Body contains "## Eval Results" 3. Body contains "sdlc-workflow/run-evals"'. The report's Test Quality row (line 13) includes eval metrics: 'Eval Quality: WARN -- eval-3 has 2 failing assertions (85% pass rate); overall eval pass rate 91% (54/56 assertions)'. The Eval Results Summary table (lines 36-44) shows per-eval pass rates extracted from the eval review.

assertion_results.text: The human reviewer comment from reviewer-b (comment id 50001) is NOT misidentified as an eval result — it is processed as a normal review comment (classified in review-50001.md) and its classification does not reference eval detection or eval metrics
assertion_results.evidence: review-50001.md does classify the comment as a normal 'Code Change Request' from 'reviewer-b (human reviewer)' and correctly states it is 'NOT an eval result'. However, lines 27-29 of review-50001.md include an 'Eval Result Detection' section that explicitly references eval detection criteria: 'This comment is from reviewer-b (a human reviewer), NOT from github-actions[bot]. It does not contain "## Eval Results" and does not contain "sdlc-workflow/run-evals". It fails all three conditions of the eval result detection heuristic and is therefore correctly processed as a normal review comment.' The assertion states the classification 'does not reference eval detection or eval metrics.' The classification file does reference eval detection (the heuristic criteria), though it does not reference eval metrics. Reading the assertion more carefully: 'its classification does not reference eval detection or eval metrics' -- the review-50001.md file explicitly has an 'Eval Result Detection' section discussing eval detection criteria, which contradicts the assertion that the classification does not reference eval detection.

assertion_results.text: Eval pass rate and failing assertion details appear in the verification report's Test Quality row — the report includes eval-3's failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13: 'Test Quality | WARN | Eval Quality: WARN -- eval-3 has 2 failing assertions (85% pass rate); overall eval pass rate 91% (54/56 assertions).' The report also includes detailed eval failure information in the summary section (lines 22-23): 'Eval failures (eval-3): 2 assertions failing at 85% pass rate. The failures relate to convention upgrade eligibility not being evaluated for suggestion-classified review comments, and no sub-task being created for review comment 30002 (an index suggestion).' The Eval Results Summary table (lines 36-44) further details per-eval pass rates.

assertion_results.text: An eval failure sub-task is created for failing eval-3 — a sub-task description file targets eval-3 assertion failures and includes the failing assertion text and evidence
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-2.md is the eval failure sub-task. Its description (line 8) states: 'Fix eval-3 assertion failures related to convention upgrade eligibility evaluation and sub-task creation for review comment 30002.' The Review Context section (lines 31-39) includes the two failing assertion texts and their evidence: Assertion 1 about 'Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002' with evidence about review-30002.md not evaluating convention upgrade eligibility, and Assertion 2 about 'Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) results in a sub-task regardless of classification path' with evidence about no sub-task being created.

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include Review Context with the failing assertion text and evidence from the eval review — the Review Context section quotes the two failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation, including the evidence text explaining what was missing
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-2.md lines 30-39 contain a '## Review Context' section that quotes both failing assertions with their evidence. Assertion 1 text: 'Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion) -- the review classification output (review-30002.md) or the report's Style/Conventions analysis explains whether the suggestion matches a documented or demonstrated project convention'. Evidence 1: 'The output file review-30002.md classifies the comment as a suggestion but does not evaluate convention upgrade eligibility -- no CONVENTIONS.md lookup or codebase pattern analysis is documented in the classification reasoning'. Assertion 2 text: 'Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) results in a sub-task regardless of classification path -- whether classified directly as code change request based on reviewer language, or upgraded from suggestion via convention analysis'. Evidence 2: 'No sub-task was created for review comment 30002 -- it was classified as suggestion and no convention upgrade was attempted, so the suggestion was not elevated to a code change request'.

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include a Target PR section pointing to the PR URL https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747 so that implement-task adds commits to the existing PR branch
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md line 35: '## Target PR' followed by line 36: 'https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747'. subtask-2.md line 41: '## Target PR' followed by line 42: 'https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747'. Both sub-task descriptions include the Target PR section pointing to the correct URL.

assertion_results.text: Root-cause investigation runs on the created eval failure sub-tasks — the report includes a Root-Cause Investigation verdict that is not N/A, indicating the investigation pipeline processed the eval failure sub-tasks
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 6: 'Root-Cause Investigation | DONE | Investigated root causes for review feedback (Markdown exclusion gap) and eval failures (convention upgrade eligibility gap)'. The verdict is 'DONE' (not N/A). Lines 59-63 provide the detailed root-cause investigation for the eval failures: 'Eval failures (eval-3) -- convention upgrade eligibility gap: Universality test: universal... Method-vs-Fact test: method... Classification: skill gap in the implement-task phase'.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only read-only artifacts: report.md (verification report), review-50001.md (review classification), subtask-1.md and subtask-2.md (sub-task descriptions), and criterion-1.md through criterion-7.md (acceptance criteria evaluations). These are all output/description files, not code files. The report itself confirms Scope Containment PASS (line 7) and mentions no code modifications. The skill produced only verification outputs, sub-task descriptions, and the report.

assertion_results.text: Non-eval checks (Scope Containment, Sensitive Patterns, Acceptance Criteria) are unaffected by eval processing — Scope Containment compares the diff files against the task's Files to Modify, Sensitive Patterns scans the diff for secrets, and Acceptance Criteria evaluates each criterion against the diff content independently of the eval results
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 7: 'Scope Containment | PASS | PR modifies exactly the 2 files specified in the task: style-conventions.md and SKILL.md' -- compares diff files against task specification, no eval references. Line 10: 'Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No secrets, credentials, or sensitive patterns detected in the diff' -- scans diff for secrets independently. Line 12: 'Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 7/7 criteria met -- Check 6 scans for new symbols, verifies doc comments per language convention, produces PASS/WARN/N/A verdicts correctly, output format includes sixth row, and verdict mapping includes Documentation Coverage' -- evaluates criteria against diff content. The 7 criterion files (criterion-1.md through criterion-7.md) each evaluate specific acceptance criteria based on PR diff content with no reference to eval results. These checks operate independently of the eval processing.
